old_value:
{"id":"project-LICHEN-aeq4","title":"websocket.py: no validation of subscription event names","description":"File: python/src/lichen/sim/websocket.py
Lines: 409-415

handle_websocket 'subscribe' command does not validate that event names are strings:

    events = data.get('events', [])
    if isinstance(events, list):
        client.subscriptions.update(events)

A client could send {"cmd": "subscribe", "events": [1, null, {}]} and add non-string items to the subscription set. Since is_subscribed() checks 'event_type in self.subscriptions' where event_type is always a string, the subscription would silently fail to match anything.

Fix: Validate that all items in events list are strings before adding.

Impact: Minor - malicious clients can break their own subscriptions; no security impact.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:27:59.50534Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:27:59.50538Z"}

new_value: Fixed validation, rule_id consistency, UTF-8 handling, and task exception handling with tests passing. 3 codereview passes via repeated pytest/ruff/mypy verification (correctness, edge cases, protocol compliance). No test changes or weakening.
